cycle_label: GOV-03 — governance-thread standalone sprint · fresh health-check sweep → health-refresh failure-signal gap · runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade

plain_english._doc: Operator-facing summary block. /brief reads this to emit a plain-language header. The governance thread is NOT a cycle — it is a thread of standalone GOV-NN sprints. cycle_number tracks the GOV sprint number (GOV-03 = 3). Re-author this block at each GOV sprint OPEN.
plain_english.summary: The governance thread runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade — not a 4th nephew. It scopes from a health-check sweep, takes ownerless cross-cutting work the cascade structurally can't absorb, and codifies findings to memory + DAcumen. GOV-02 closed fully (carryover backlog was rot + CLEARED; casey-pipeline standing watch resolved in L04). GOV-03 opened from a fresh health-check sweep (L01) and found the headline: the health-refresh cron fails silently — 6 user-crontab jobs curl into casey-pipeline :8912 with output discarded, so when the pipeline is down every refresh fails with zero signal. That is why GOV-02 found 24-day-stale health scores with nothing surfacing it. GOV-03's job is to give that refresh mechanism a failure signal.
goals: L01 — fresh health-check sweep. DONE. Headline: health-refresh cron has no failure signal (structural hole #3, purest form yet). Also found: project-health-reconcile.json is an orphaned artifact (24d stale, no producer).

L02 — queue #3 NCAA baseball archived per operator decision. DONE. All 4 baseball unit files moved to ~/.config/systemd/user/archived-2026-05-14/; darnometer code + DB left intact (darnometer-owned, out of GOV scope).

L03 — execute queue #1: give the 6 health-refresh cron jobs a liveness/failure signal (logging wrapper, last-success healthcheck, or stop discarding non-200s). Fold in #2 verify-and-strike of the orphaned reconcile artifact.
Watch: #4 dellatech-rag-indexer never run → re-check after first scheduled fire 05-15 02:32
decisions_pending_operator: GOV-03 L03 in progress — executing the health-refresh failure-signal fix (#1) + verify-and-strike of the orphaned reconcile artifact (#2). No operator decision pending.
plain_english.proposed_next_action: GOV-03 L01 (sweep) and L02 (NCAA baseball archive, operator-directed) are closed. L03 is the headline work: give the health-refresh cron a failure signal so a dead pipeline can't silently freeze every health score again, and fold in #2's verify-and-strike of the orphaned project-health-reconcile.json. #4 stays on watch.

cycle_mode_notes: GOV sprints do not pillar-rotate and do not join the cascade. cascade_mode is standalone by design — the three-sprint cascade is capped at 3 (three is the floor AND the ceiling). GOV takes ownerless cross-cutting work, never nephew COLLECT-queue work.
structure: Health-check sweep scopes the queue → GOV executes the ownerless backlog as a standalone sprint → findings codified to memory + DAcumen
cascade_mode: standalone — runs ABOVE the three-sprint cascade, not a 4th nephew
authored_by: operator-direct — GOV-03 opened after GOV-02 closed fully (queue + casey-pipeline standing watch both resolved); scoped from a fresh health-check sweep per the operating model
stakes: The governance thread is cross-BU and standalone — nesting its sprint-logs inside one BU's foreman (darntech) stranded them when the active cycle moved, and made /brief unrunnable from the governance workspace. Its own foreman-enabled home makes the thread continuously briefable and parallel-runnable alongside whatever product cycle is active.
notes: GOV-02 closed 2026-05-14 at a genuine full-stop — carryover backlog was entirely rot (CLEARED), casey-pipeline standing watch resolved in L04 (RC6 landed but dev venv unreconciled; GOV swapped the stale rag client). GOV-03 opened the same day from a fresh L01 health-check sweep. The governance-thread workspace is a checkout of dacumen.git — governance artifacts (charter amendments, ADRs) and governance operational tracking (.foreman + GOV sprint-logs) both belong to the dacumen repo.

carryover_decisions_at_open.dellatech_rag_indexer_watch: GOV-03 L01 sweep found dellatech-rag-indexer.service has never run (LAST = -). Likely just newly installed — it is static+timer-triggered, first scheduled fire Fri 2026-05-15 02:32. Re-check after that; if it still shows LAST = - it is a real finding for a later GOV loop.
carryover_decisions_at_open.gov_04_scoping: GOV-04 must scope from a fresh health-check sweep per feedback_governance_thread_standalone_sprints.md — run the check first, don't scope from a hunch.
